title: Error-prone PCR (Random Mutagenesis)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"85bnf","text":"2023 NUS-Singapore iGEM Team followed this protocol to introduce random mutation in DNA fragments. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9furk","text":"Proper lab PPE must be worn at all times.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add the primers, the DNA template, and the following reagents from the GeneMorph II Random Mutagenesis Kits Agilent Technologies Catalog #200550 into a PCR tube to make a 50 µL PCR sample: %s %s DI water 41.5μL 10x Mutazyme II (10x Reaction Buffer) 5μL 40mM dNTP Mix 1μL Each Primer (both forward & reverse) 0.5μL Mutazyme II 1μL Template 0.25μL

Place the sample into the Thermal Cycler and set it with the following conditions: Purpose Temperature Duration 1 Initial Denaturation 95°C 2 minutes 2 Denaturation 95°C 1 minute 3 Annealing 55°C 1 minute 4 Extension 72°C 1 minute 5 Go to step 2, repeat the cycle 44 times 6 Extension 72°C 10 minutes 7 Finish 12°C Infinite Loop
Upon finishing the PCR steps, add 10 µL of DNA loading dye.
Proceeds to the gel electrophoresis to isolate the gene fragment of interest.
creator: NUS iGEM
